generatedSentence: Halfway across the offshore rig, a homesick bachelor, typically passive, realized his grandfather's intact, important heirloom ‚ÄĒ a sticky, ionic tacticity display ‚ÄĒ was still able to engage the system. He tacked an understatement about its gravity to a hidden website, knowing impeccable improvisation would be his guide when his dryad brother needed entry. Suddenly, radioactive raids rocked the factory, a maniac criminal's unbreakable monopoly on crypto now challenged; he'd rejected a verification replay, deeming it a bummer. A towhead swashbuckler, swift as a wasp, found his technical takedown offside, feeling a potent, rabid objection to the staggering judgement. Under cinematic lighting, a feminine librarian, her upswept hair catching the brightness, watched with a pucker of concern as the truck, now buckling from shocks, crashed through a checkpoint. She knew the junker held doubly dangerous whiskies, a dangerous tarantula, and explosive oranges, a testament to the artists' exhumed drawings. From the aircraft, a volunteer vlogger, reeling from a flashback of childhood minuet, saw the lookout pound a sticker onto a shacky wall. The doggy, sensing infinite volume of urine, avoided the rowlock; this was no chicken raffle, but a cosmic apology and a demonstration of cosmic diffraction, a true embrace beyond the mackles.
panelSentences: Halfway across the offshore rig, a homesick bachelor, typically passive, realized his grandfather's intact, important heirloom ‚ÄĒ a sticky, ionic tacticity display ‚ÄĒ was still able to engage the system. He tacked an understatement about its gravity to a hidden website, knowing impeccable improvisation would be his guide when his dryad brother needed entry.

Suddenly, radioactive raids rocked the factory, a maniac criminal's unbreakable monopoly on crypto now challenged; he'd rejected a verification replay, deeming it a bummer. A towhead swashbuckler, swift as a wasp, found his technical takedown offside, feeling a potent, rabid objection to the staggering judgement.

Under cinematic lighting, a feminine librarian, her upswept hair catching the brightness, watched with a pucker of concern as the truck, now buckling from shocks, crashed through a checkpoint. She knew the junker held doubly dangerous whiskies, a dangerous tarantula, and explosive oranges, a testament to the artists' exhumed drawings.

From the aircraft, a volunteer vlogger, reeling from a flashback of childhood minuet, saw the lookout pound a sticker onto a shacky wall. The doggy, sensing infinite volume of urine, avoided the rowlock; this was no chicken raffle, but a cosmic apology and a demonstration of cosmic diffraction, a true embrace beyond the mackles.
rawPanelSentences: Halfway across the offshore rig, a homesick bachelor, typically passive, realized his grandfather's intact, important heirloom ‚ÄĒ a sticky, ionic tacticity display ‚ÄĒ was still able to engage the system. He tacked an understatement about its gravity to a hidden website, knowing impeccable improvisation would be his guide when his dryad brother needed entry.

Suddenly, radioactive raids rocked the factory, a maniac criminal's unbreakable monopoly on crypto now challenged; he'd rejected a verification replay, deeming it a bummer. A towhead swashbuckler, swift as a wasp, found his technical takedown offside, feeling a potent, rabid objection to the staggering judgement.

Under cinematic lighting, a feminine librarian, her upswept hair catching the brightness, watched with a pucker of concern as the truck, now buckling from shocks, crashed through a checkpoint. She knew the junker held doubly dangerous whiskies, a dangerous tarantula, and explosive oranges, a testament to the artists' exhumed drawings.

From the aircraft, a volunteer vlogger, reeling from a flashback of childhood minuet, saw the lookout pound a sticker onto a shacky wall. The doggy, sensing infinite volume of urine, avoided the rowlock; this was no chicken raffle, but a cosmic apology and a demonstration of cosmic diffraction, a true embrace beyond the mackles.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A cybernetic swashbuckler wearing a tattered trench coat and glowing blue optical implants. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure, the cybernetic swashbuckler, standing halfway on a precarious offshore rig, looking homesick, with a holographic display glowing. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The swashbuckler, mid-takedown, grappling with a maniac criminal in a radioactive factory, sparks flying. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A feminine librarian with upswept hair, witnessing a futuristic truck crashing through a checkpoint, debris scattering. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cybernetic swashbuckler, looking up at an aircraft, an animal companion nearby, as a volunteer vlogger records from above.

definitions.definition: Half of the way between two points; midway.

To seize (something) eagerly or with alacrity; to accept or take up with cordiality; to welcome.

definitions.definition: To submit to; to undergo.
To encircle; to enclose, to encompass.
To enfold, to include (ideas, principles, etc.); to encompass.

definitions.definition: To fasten on, as armour.
To accept (someone) as a friend; to accept (someone's) help gladly.
To attempt to influence (a court, jury, etc.) corruptly; to practise embracery.
A pivot attached to the gunwale (outrigger in a sport boat) of a boat that supports and guides an oar, and provides a fulcrum for rowing; an oarlock (mostly US).
Any of various edible European bivalve mollusks, of the family Cardiidae, having heart-shaped shells.
The shell of such a mollusk.
(in the plural) One‚Äôs innermost feelings (only in the expression ‚Äúthe cockles of one‚Äôs heart‚ÄĚ).
(directly from French coquille) A wrinkle, pucker
(by extension) A defect in sheepskin; firm dark nodules caused by the bites of keds on live sheep
The mineral black tourmaline or schorl.
The fire chamber of a furnace.
A kiln for drying hops; an oast.
The dome of a heating furnace.
To cause to contract into wrinkles or ridges, as some kinds of cloth after a wetting; to pucker.
Any of several field weeds, such as the common corncockle (Agrostemma githago) and darnel ryegrass (Lolium temulentum).
A ¬£10 note; a tenner.
A point or place where a check is performed, especially a point along a road or on a frontier where travellers are stopped for inspection
definitions.example: The travellers were stopped at the checkpoint.

definitions.definition: A situation, often represented by a point in time, at which the state of a database system is known to be valid, and to which it can be returned in the event of a crisis by using a combination of backups and logs; the data stored at this event.
A predetermined point in a map, level or scenario that the player may resume from if they die or restart from if they choose to.
definitions.example: You can't finish the race if you haven't passed all of the checkpoints on the track.

definitions.definition: To set a checkpoint.
A young German noble or squire, especially a member of the aristocratic party in Prussia, stereotyped with narrow-minded militaristic and authoritarian attitudes.
(usually of relative importance, of degree, quantity or measure) In a double manner; twice the severity or degree.
definitions.example: My mother was always doubly careful when winding the grandfather clock.

definitions.definition: In two ways
definitions.example: Stealing and then lying about it is doubly wrong.

definitions.definition: With duplicity
A spot.
A blur or an appearance of a double impression, as when the paper slips a little during printing.
To blur or be blurred; especially to blur or double an impression from type.
A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift
The study of the physical universe beyond the Earth's atmosphere, including the process of mapping locations and properties of the matter and radiation in the universe.
Astrology.
Perfect, without faults, flaws or errors
definitions.example: He grew up in Norway, but he writes impeccable English.

definitions.definition: Incapable of wrongdoing or sin; immaculate
Of, relating to, or containing ions
To adjust so as to make as flat or perpendicular to the ground as possible.
definitions.example: You can level the table by turning the pads that screw into the feet.

definitions.definition: To destroy by reducing to ground level; to raze.
definitions.example: The hurricane leveled the forest.

definitions.definition: To progress to the next level.
definitions.example: I levelled after defeating the dragon.

definitions.definition: To aim or direct (a weapon, a stare, an accusation, etc).
definitions.example: He levelled an accusation of fraud at the directors.‚ÄÉ The hunter levels the gun before taking a shot.

definitions.definition: To direct or impose (a penalty, fine, etc) at or upon (someone).
To make the score of a game equal.
To bring to a common level or plane, in respect of rank, condition, character, privilege, etc.
definitions.example: to level all the ranks and conditions of men

definitions.definition: To adjust or adapt to a certain level.
definitions.example: to level remarks to the capacity of children

definitions.definition: (usually with "with") To speak honestly and openly with.
The process of making something level.
The process of measuring levels to establish heights and altitudes.
To dig out of the ground; to take out of a place of burial; to disinter.
definitions.example: The archeologist exhumed artifacts from the ground with a shovel.

definitions.definition: To uncover; to bring to light.
A contributor to a vlog.
An incomplete statement, particularly:
To nail with a tack (small nail with a flat head).
To sew/stich with a tack (loose seam used to temporarily fasten pieces of cloth).
To maneuver a sailing vessel so that its bow turns through the wind, i.e. the wind changes from one side of the vessel to the other.
To add something as an extra item.

definitions.definition: Often paired with "up", to place the tack on a horse.
A collection of interlinked web pages on the World Wide Web that are typically accessible from the same base URL and reside on the same server.
A show or spectacle.
definitions.example: The trapeze artist put on an amazing acrobatic display.

definitions.definition: A piece of work to be presented visually.
definitions.example: Pupils are expected to produce a wall display about a country of their choice.

definitions.definition: An electronic screen that shows graphics or text.
The presentation of information for visual or tactile reception.
(travel, in a reservation system) The asterisk symbol, used to denote that the following information will be displayed, eg, *H will "display history".
To show conspicuously; to exhibit; to demonstrate; to manifest.
To make a display; to act as one making a show or demonstration.
To extend the front of (a column), bringing it into line.
To make conspicuous by using large or prominent type.
To discover; to descry.
To spread out, to unfurl.
In Greek myth, a female tree spirit.
Son of the same parents as another person.
A male having at least one parent in common with another (see half-brother, stepbrother).
A male fellow member of a religious community, church, trades union etc.
definitions.example: I would like to thank the brother who just spoke.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man.
definitions.example: Listen, brother, I don't know what you want, but I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A black male.
Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause or situation.
Someone who is a peer, whether male or female.
To treat as a brother.
Expressing exasperation.
definitions.example: We're being forced to work overtime? Oh, brother!

definitions.definition: The clang of a large bell.
Doorbell chimes.
To pull a bell.
To ring a doorbell.
Sway unsteadily, reel, or totter.
Doubt, waver, be shocked.
Have multiple groups doing the same thing in a uniform fashion, but starting at different, evenly-spaced, times or places (attested from 1856).
The motion of one who staggers.
That which staggers something or somebody.
In animation, the repetition of a sequence of frames to show struggling effort
Incredible, overwhelming, amazing.

definitions.definition: A sudden, heavy impact.

definitions.definition: A discontinuity arising in the solution of a partial differential equation.

definitions.definition: To give an electric shock to.
To meet with a shock; to collide in a violent encounter.
An arrangement of sheaves for drying; a stook.
A lot consisting of sixty pieces; a term applied in some Baltic ports to loose goods.
(by extension) A tuft or bunch of something, such as hair or grass.
definitions.example: His head boasted a shock of sandy hair.

definitions.definition: A small dog with long shaggy hair, especially a poodle or spitz; a shaggy lapdog.
To collect, or make up, into a shock or shocks; to stook.
definitions.example: to shock rye

definitions.definition: An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.
The fruit of an orange tree; a citrus fruit with a slightly sour flavour.
The colour of a ripe fruit of an orange tree, midway between red and yellow.
Orange juice.
Orange coloured and flavoured cordial.
Orange coloured and flavoured soft drink.
To color orange.
To become orange.
Having the colour of the fruit of an orange tree; yellowred; reddish-yellow.
The keeper, manager of a library.
One who cares for the publications, files etc. in a library, whether staff or volunteer.
A person who processes and organizes information.
A vantage point with a view of the surrounding area.
A session of watching for an approaching enemy, police, etc.

definitions.definition: A person on watch for approaching enemy, police, danger, etc.
definitions.example: The raid failed when the lookout noticed the enemy group.

definitions.definition: A subject for observation; a prospect or view.
One's perspective, outlook; hence, one's responsibility. (used with a possessive pronoun or a noun in a possessive form).
definitions.example: Every man's interest is his own lookout.

definitions.definition: A fold or wrinkle.
A state of perplexity or anxiety; confusion; bother; agitation.
To pinch or wrinkle; to squeeze inwardly, to dimple or fold.
A unit of mass equal to 16 avoirdupois ounces (= 453.592 37 g). Today this value is the most common meaning of "pound" as a unit of weight.
A unit of mass equal to 12 troy ounces (‚Čą 373.242 g). Today, this is a common unit of weight when measuring precious metals, and is little used elsewhere.
The symbol # (octothorpe, hash)
The unit of currency used in the United Kingdom and its dependencies. It is divided into 100 pence.
Any of various units of currency used in Egypt and Lebanon, and formerly in the Republic of Ireland, Cyprus and Israel.
Any of various units of currency formerly used in the United States.

definitions.definition: Abbreviation for pound-force, a unit of force/weight. Using this abbreviation to describe pound-force is inaccurate and unscientific.
A male peafowl, especially Pavo cristatus, notable for its brilliant iridescently ocellated tail.
A peafowl (of the genus Pavo or Afropavo), either male or female.
A vainglorious person .
Any of various Asian species of papilionid butterflies of the genus Papilio.
To strut about proudly.
To engage in peacocking, ostentatious dress or behaviour to impress women.
Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.

definitions.definition: A document or book that offers information or instruction; guidebook.
A sign that guides people; guidepost.
Any marking or object that catches the eye to provide quick reference.
A device that guides part of a machine, or guides motion or action.
A spirit believed to speak through a medium.
A member of a group marching in formation who sets the pattern of movement or alignment for the rest.
A drawing, often held as a fundraiser, in which tickets or chances are sold to win a prize.

definitions.definition: A game of dice in which the player who throws three of the same number wins all the stakes.
To award something by means of a raffle or random drawing, often used with off.
definitions.example: They raffled off four gift baskets.

definitions.example: to raffle for a watch

definitions.definition: That which is feminine.
(possibly obsolete) A woman.
(grammar) The feminine gender.

Of or pertaining to the female gender; womanly.
Of or pertaining to the female sex; biologically female, not male.
Belonging to females; typically used by females.

definitions.definition: Having the qualities stereotypically associated with women: nurturing, not aggressive.
(grammar) Of, pertaining or belonging to the female grammatical gender, in languages that have gender distinctions.
(authorship) A dramatic device in which an earlier event is inserted into the normal chronological flow of a narrative.

A similar recurrence of the effects of a hallucinogenic drug.
The condition of the flame propagating down the hose of an oxy-fuel welding system.
A query that operates against data from an earlier time, before it was changed.
To undergo a flashback; to experience a vivid mental image from the past.
To turn by means of a crank.

definitions.definition: (of a crank or similar) To turn.
definitions.example: He's been cranking all day and yet it refuses to crank.

definitions.definition: To cause to spin via other means, as though turned by a crank.

definitions.definition: To act in a cranky manner; to behave unreasonably and irritably, especially through complaining.
definitions.example: Quit cranking about your spilt milk!

definitions.definition: To be running at a high level of output or effort.
definitions.example: By one hour into the shift, the boys were really cranking.

definitions.definition: To run with a winding course; to double; to crook; to wind and turn.
Formed with, or having, a bend or crank.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of juggle; one who either literally juggles objects, or figuratively juggles tasks.
A person who practices juggling.
A person who performs tricks using sleight of hand, a conjurer, prestidigitator.
A magician or wizard.
One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.

One who enters into military service voluntarily, but who, when in service, is subject to discipline and regulations like other soldiers; -- opposed to conscript; specifically, a voluntary member of the organized militia of a country as distinguished from the standing army.
A person who acts out of his own will without a legal obligation, such as a donor.
A plant that grows spontaneously, without being cultivated on purpose; see volunteer plant in Wikipedia.
A native or resident of the American state of Tennessee.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.
To do or offer to do something voluntarily.

definitions.definition: To offer, usually unprompted.
definitions.example: to volunteer an explanation

definitions.definition: To grow without human sowing or intentional cultivation.
To offer the services of (someone else) to do something.

definitions.definition: A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
definitions.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

definitions.definition: Strength of sound; loudness.
definitions.example: Please turn down the volume on the stereo.

definitions.definition: The issues of a periodical over a period of one year.
definitions.example: I looked at this week's copy of the magazine. It was volume 23, issue 45.

definitions.definition: A bound book.
A single book of a publication issued in multi-book format, such as an encyclopedia.
definitions.example: The letter "G" was found in volume 4.

definitions.definition: A roll or scroll, which was the form of ancient books.
Quantity.

definitions.definition: A rounded mass or convolution.
The total supply of money in circulation or, less frequently, total amount of credit extended, within a specified national market or worldwide.
An accessible storage area with a single file system, typically resident on a single partition of a hard disk.
To be conveyed through the air, waft.
To cause to move through the air, waft.
To swell.
Liquid waste consisting of water, salts and urea, which is made in the kidneys, stored in the bladder, then released through the urethra.
To urinate.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.
definitions.example: Mexican open-bed trucks haul most of the fresh produce that comes into the United States from Mexico.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.
A garden cart, a two-wheeled wheelbarrow.
A small wagon or cart, of various designs, pushed or pulled by hand or pulled by an animal, as with those in hotels for moving luggage, or in libraries for transporting books.
A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.
A platform with wheels or casters.
Dirt or other messiness.
To drive a truck: Generally a truck driver's slang.
To convey by truck.
definitions.example: Last week, Cletus trucked 100 pounds of lumber up to Dubuque.

definitions.definition: To travel or live contentedly.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist, to endure.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: (film production) To move a camera parallel to the movement of the subject.
To fight or otherwise physically engage with.
To run over or through a tackler in American football.
The relative stereochemistry of adjacent chiral centers within a macromolecule.
Any of many types of stinging flying insect resembling a hornet.
Any of the members of suborder Apocrita, excepting the ants (family Formicidae) and bees (clade Anthophila).
A person who behaves in an angry or insolent way, hence waspish.
